title: US 250th anniversary celebrations mark a day of contrasts between political speeches and weather phenomena
summary: The anniversary of American independence was accompanied by a heatwave, messages from Putin and the Pope, and fiery speeches from Trump, reflecting current polarization and challenges.

title: Cape Verde, the World Cup revelation, receives praise for its performance against the champion
summary: The debutant African team competed on equal terms against the reigning champion, earning the respect of the fans and the media for their dedication and offensive play.

title: Massive funeral of former supreme leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei, amid regional tensions
summary: Thousands of people attended the funeral ceremony in Tehran, where chants against the US and Israel were heard, reaffirming loyalty to the Iranian regime.

title: Political crisis in Japan: Parliament blocked and low approval of laws
summary: The ruling coalition faces difficulties in advancing the legislative agenda, with historically low approval ratings and the opposition demanding the prime minister's appearance.

title: Japan defeats Italy in its debut in the rugby Nations Cup
summary: The Japanese team showed solid and effective play to defeat Italy in the opening match of the new international rugby tournament.

title: Six years since the devastating floods in Kyushu, not forgetting the victims
summary: Families and authorities remembered those killed in the 2020 floods, especially in the nursing home where 14 people died, as communities still recover.

title: China intensifies maritime presence in disputed waters and criticizes Japan-India cooperation
summary: Beijing has increased patrols near Taiwan and the Senkaku Islands, while warning against greater security cooperation between Tokyo and New Delhi.

title: Ukraine attacks a refinery in Russia while Moscow claims advances on the eastern front
summary: In an escalation of the conflict, Kyiv launched a drone attack on an oil facility in St. Petersburg, while the Russian army says it has captured a Ukrainian stronghold.

title: Japanese companies incorporate artificial intelligence to optimize operations and address labor shortages
summary: From train ticket counter service to rescue management and autonomous vehicles, AI is making its way into various productive sectors in Japan.

title: Professional baseball roundup in Japan: SoftBank's streak ends, Chunichi and Lotte triumph
summary: The SoftBank Hawks saw their six-game winning streak end, while the Chunichi Dragons and Lotte Marines achieved important victories.

title: The World Cup defines the round of 16 with the three host countries qualified
summary: Canada, the United States and Mexico advanced to the next round, while Haiti and other teams seek to make history in the international tournament.

title: Heavy rain alert in northern Kyushu for the weekend
summary: Weather authorities warn about the possibility of intense and continuous rainfall, with risk of floods and landslides.

title: Lionel Messi reaches 20 goals in eight consecutive games and remains unstoppable
summary: The Argentine star continues to demonstrate his quality by scoring in every match and being decisive in his team's results.

title: Harassment allegations in a Fuji TV drama generate controversy and mixed reactions
summary: The accusations of sexual harassment against an actor have sparked an intense debate on social media, with contradictory responses from the parties involved.

title: Wimbledon 2025: defending champion Swiatek eliminated and Wawrinka dazzles in the London night
summary: In the world's oldest tennis tournament, Poland's Iga Swiatek was surprised in an early round, while Switzerland's Stan Wawrinka offered a memorable performance.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi receives the 'Jewelry Best Dresser Award'
summary: The Japanese leader attended the award ceremony wearing a pearl necklace that belonged to her mother, at an event that promotes the jewelry industry.

title: Japan suffers a heatwave that affects productivity and boosts sales of cooling products
summary: Extreme temperatures are causing labor losses estimated in billions of hours, while consumers seek relief in items such as ice hats.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela leaves more than 2,600 dead and criticism over lack of assistance
summary: The large-magnitude earthquake has caused a humanitarian tragedy, with a shortage of machinery to remove debris and allegations of abandonment by the government.

title: Tibetan self-immolates in front of UN headquarters to protest Chinese ethnic unity law
summary: An ethnic Tibetan man set himself on fire in New York to denounce Beijing's cultural assimilation policies, joining a long list of similar protests.

title: Japan records historic tax revenue of 84 trillion yen
summary: Tax collection reached an unprecedented figure in fiscal year 2025, driven by economic growth and the increase in consumption taxes.
